report_metadata.title: Aegis Migration Factory — SOC-2 Type II Compliance Assessment

executive_summary:
This SOC-2 Type II compliance assessment evaluates the GCP-to-AWS infrastructure migration performed by the Aegis Migration Factory. The migration scope encompasses 22 GCP resources translated to 20 AWS equivalents across compute, storage, database, networking, serverless, and messaging service categories.

The overall compliance score of 78/100 reflects strong implementation of access controls (CC6) and change management (CC8), with areas for improvement in system operations monitoring (CC7) and availability controls (A1). The migration process successfully addressed several pre-existing security vulnerabilities including over-permissioned IAM roles (roles/editor eliminated), public storage bucket exposure (CMEK encryption added), and deprecated infrastructure components (N1 machine types upgraded).

Key strengths include: zero-trust IAM implementation with 8 task-specific policies replacing 2 overly-broad GCP bindings, KMS encryption on all storage resources, deletion protection on production databases, and proper network segmentation via VPC with public/private subnet isolation.

Areas requiring attention: MFA enforcement on privileged roles, CloudTrail configuration for comprehensive audit logging, formal incident response procedures, and documented recovery objectives (RPO/RTO) for business continuity planning.

CC6.1.evidence: IAM roles with least-privilege policies implemented for all 20 AWS resources. Each role has specific trust policies and permission boundaries. Security groups restrict network access by port and CIDR range.

CC6.2.evidence: All IAM roles use service-linked trust policies requiring AWS STS AssumeRole. No static credentials are provisioned. EC2 instances use instance profiles rather than access keys.

CC6.3.evidence: Role-based access implemented with 8 separate IAM roles matching workload requirements. Original GCP roles/editor (project-wide) decomposed into task-specific policies with exact actions and resource ARNs.

CC6.6.evidence: VPC with public/private subnet isolation. Security groups defined for web and internal traffic patterns. KMS encryption on storage resources.
gaps: No AWS WAF configured for application-layer protection
No VPC Flow Logs enabled for network monitoring
recommendations: Deploy AWS WAF on the application load balancer
Enable VPC Flow Logs and ship to CloudWatch Logs

CC6.7.evidence: KMS encryption at rest for S3 buckets and RDS. S3 bucket policies enforce HTTPS-only access (deny aws:SecureTransport=false).
gaps: No ACM certificate configured for in-transit encryption on load balancer
Redis ElastiCache in-transit encryption not explicitly enabled
recommendations: Configure ACM certificates for TLS termination
Enable in-transit encryption on ElastiCache Redis cluster

CC6.8.evidence: No public S3 buckets (corrected from GCP). Security group ingress rules restrict access. RDS instances in private subnet with security group protection. No IAM policies with wildcard actions or resources.

CC7.1.evidence: Lambda function logs to CloudWatch. EKS cluster has basic Kubernetes logging.
gaps: No AWS Inspector configured for vulnerability scanning
No Amazon GuardDuty enabled for threat detection
No automated patching schedule defined
recommendations: Enable AWS Inspector for continuous vulnerability assessment
Enable GuardDuty for intelligent threat detection
Configure Systems Manager Patch Manager for automated OS patching

CC7.2.evidence: CloudWatch basic monitoring enabled on EC2 instances. RDS has enhanced monitoring available.
gaps: No CloudWatch alarms configured for critical metrics
No centralized logging solution (CloudWatch Logs Insights or ELK)
recommendations: Create CloudWatch alarms for CPU, memory, disk, and network anomalies
Implement centralized log aggregation with CloudWatch Logs Insights

CC7.3.evidence: No formal security event evaluation procedures defined in the infrastructure.
gaps: No AWS SecurityHub enabled
No automated security event triage workflow
No integration with incident management system
recommendations: Enable AWS SecurityHub for unified security posture management
Create EventBridge rules for automated security event routing
Integrate with PagerDuty or OpsGenie for incident management

gaps: No documented incident response plan
No automated containment procedures
No forensic investigation capabilities
recommendations: Document incident response procedures and runbooks
Create Lambda-based automated containment actions
Enable CloudTrail with S3 logging for forensic analysis

CC7.5.evidence: RDS automated backups with 7-day retention. S3 versioning on backup bucket.
gaps: No documented recovery procedures
No regular disaster recovery testing
recommendations: Document and test RDS point-in-time recovery procedures
Implement quarterly disaster recovery exercises

CC8.1.evidence: All infrastructure defined as Terraform code (Infrastructure as Code). Changes require terraform plan/apply workflow. State file stored in S3 with DynamoDB locking. Full migration documented by Aegis pipeline with audit trail.
recommendations: Add Terraform Sentinel policies for policy-as-code enforcement

A1.1.evidence: EKS auto-scaling configured (2-10 nodes). RDS Multi-AZ deployment enabled.
gaps: No EC2 Auto Scaling group for app/worker instances
No defined capacity planning process
recommendations: Migrate standalone EC2 instances to Auto Scaling groups
Implement capacity planning with AWS Compute Optimizer

A1.2.evidence: RDS Multi-AZ provides database failover. EKS distributes across availability zones.
gaps: No multi-region disaster recovery plan
No Route53 health checks for failover
recommendations: Design multi-region DR strategy with RTO/RPO targets
Implement Route53 health checks and failover routing

A1.3.evidence: RDS automated backups exist but no documented RPO/RTO objectives.
gaps: No defined Recovery Point Objective (RPO)
No defined Recovery Time Objective (RTO)
No backup verification testing
recommendations: Define RPO ≤ 1 hour and RTO ≤ 4 hours for production workloads
Implement automated backup restoration testing
Create runbook for full environment recovery

findings.title: Missing MFA enforcement on privileged IAM roles
findings.description: No aws:MultiFactorAuthPresent condition on IAM roles with write permissions to production resources (RDS, S3, KMS). An attacker with stolen role credentials could modify or delete production data.
findings.recommendation: Add Condition: {"Bool": {"aws:MultiFactorAuthPresent": "true"}} to all policies with write actions on production resources

findings.title: No CloudTrail audit logging configured
findings.description: AWS CloudTrail is not explicitly configured in the Terraform infrastructure. Without CloudTrail, API calls to AWS services are not logged, preventing security investigations and compliance auditing.
findings.recommendation: Enable CloudTrail in all regions with multi-region trail, S3 log delivery, and CloudWatch Logs integration

findings.title: No defined RPO/RTO for disaster recovery
findings.description: While RDS automated backups are enabled, there are no formally defined Recovery Point Objective or Recovery Time Objective targets. Recovery procedures have not been tested.
findings.recommendation: Define RPO ≤ 1 hour and RTO ≤ 4 hours. Implement and test quarterly DR exercises.

findings.title: Web security group allows unrestricted inbound HTTP/HTTPS
findings.description: Security group aws_security_group.web allows inbound traffic from 0.0.0.0/0 on ports 80 and 443. This was migrated from the GCP firewall rule and should be fronted by a WAF.
findings.recommendation: Deploy AWS WAF with rate limiting and common exploit protection. Consider using CloudFront for DDoS mitigation.

findings.title: ElastiCache Redis in-transit encryption not enabled
findings.description: Redis cache cluster does not have transit_encryption_enabled set to true. Data transmitted between application and cache is unencrypted.
findings.recommendation: Enable transit_encryption_enabled and at_rest_encryption_enabled on the ElastiCache cluster

findings.title: No VPC Flow Logs configured
findings.description: VPC Flow Logs are not enabled, preventing network traffic analysis and anomaly detection.
findings.recommendation: Enable VPC Flow Logs with delivery to CloudWatch Logs or S3 for retention and analysis

findings.title: No automated patching for EC2 instances
findings.description: Standalone EC2 instances (app_server, worker) do not have AWS Systems Manager Patch Manager configured for automated security patching.
findings.recommendation: Install SSM Agent and configure Patch Manager with maintenance windows for automated patching

findings.title: Consider migrating standalone EC2 to Auto Scaling Groups
findings.description: App server and worker are single EC2 instances without auto-scaling or self-healing capabilities. For production workloads, Auto Scaling Groups provide better availability.
findings.recommendation: Migrate to Launch Templates with Auto Scaling Groups for improved resilience

risk_matrix.description: Unauthorized access to production database via compromised IAM credentials
risk_matrix.mitigation: RDS in private subnet with security group restrictions. IAM role has only rds-db:connect permission.

risk_matrix.description: Data exfiltration from S3 media bucket
risk_matrix.mitigation: KMS encryption, bucket policy denying HTTP access, IAM roles with specific bucket ARN restrictions

risk_matrix.description: DDoS attack on public-facing web security group
risk_matrix.mitigation: Security group limits to ports 80/443 only. No WAF or DDoS protection currently configured.

risk_matrix.description: Extended downtime due to EKS cluster failure without documented recovery
risk_matrix.mitigation: EKS multi-AZ node distribution. No documented recovery procedures.

risk_matrix.description: Compliance gap from missing audit trail (no CloudTrail)
risk_matrix.mitigation: No current mitigation. CloudTrail must be enabled.

risk_matrix.description: Data loss from database corruption or accidental deletion
risk_matrix.mitigation: RDS automated backups (7-day retention), deletion_protection enabled, Multi-AZ deployment

recommendations: CRITICAL: Enable AWS CloudTrail in all regions with S3 log delivery and CloudWatch integration
HIGH: Add MFA condition to all IAM policies with write permissions on production resources
HIGH: Define RPO ≤ 1 hour, RTO ≤ 4 hours, and implement quarterly DR testing
MEDIUM: Deploy AWS WAF on the application endpoint with OWASP Top 10 rule set
MEDIUM: Enable VPC Flow Logs for network traffic analysis and anomaly detection
MEDIUM: Enable in-transit encryption on ElastiCache Redis cluster
MEDIUM: Enable AWS SecurityHub for unified security posture management
MEDIUM: Configure AWS GuardDuty for intelligent threat detection
LOW: Implement AWS Systems Manager Patch Manager for automated OS patching
LOW: Set up AWS Config rules for continuous compliance monitoring
INFO: Consider migrating standalone EC2 instances to Auto Scaling Groups for improved availability
